open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's new module (burst.js TASK-357) is NOT a new ingest boundary — it reads ONLY the ALREADY-OPEN, already-validated document via currentDoc.getData(), builds fresh per-page pdf-lib docs (open doc never mutated), and uploads nothing. It IS a bounded client-side output-amplifier: it explodes the doc into one single-page PDF per selected page (each page carries copied resources, so total bytes can exceed the source), buffers every single-page PDF's bytes in an entries[] array, then concatenates them into one ZIP Uint8Array — so peak memory roughly = sum of all single-page PDFs + the combined ZIP. Same LOW self-DoS family as the other per-page consumers (uploader's own tab only, no server impact), bounded by the same single fix — the viewer-load page-count cap. Pages are processed sequentially (await each save), which keeps the per-page work serial. No new boundary, no separate cap needed beyond the viewer-load cap. All remaining gaps are client-side-only DoS on the 1.6 GiB box; no server-side risk. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles().
open_gaps.SEC-CSP-FRAME: INFO. frame-src 'self' blob: in the live CSP (added 2026-06-13 for the Print tool, print.js). Allows only same-origin + same-origin-blob frames, no remote origins; frame-ancestors 'self' unchanged. Verified live this tick.

notes: 47 JS modules (+1 since the 2026-06-13 12:20 review: burst.js TASK-357). burst.js is security CLEAN and of the same one-shot page-tool class as the prior batch (flip/nup/margins/booklet): no new upload boundary (operates on the open viewer doc's own bytes via pdf.js doc.getData(), builds fresh per-page pdf-lib docs, open doc never mutated, nothing leaves the browser); filename sanitized [^a-zA-Z0-9._-]->_ slice(180) -> <base>-pages.zip; ZIP entry names are app-generated ASCII numeric only (page-NNN.pdf — no path traversal, no user string in entry names); status via textContent only (never innerHTML); safe Blob download with rel=noopener + setTimeout URL.revokeObjectURL(1s); guards for no-doc/numPages==0 and window.PDFLib-unready; errors caught -> inline status + EventBus.ERROR; talks to the app only through EventBus (PDF_LOADED/PDF_CLEARED) + ActionRegistry. parseRange() strictly validates the page-range input (rejects <1, >max, malformed tokens with specific messages; never silent). NOTABLE (not a defect): the TASK-357 idea-maker/PM notes claimed JSZip was 'already-in-stack (lib/jszip.min.js)', but JSZip is in fact NOT present in lib/ (confirmed: no jszip.min.js in repo web/lib/ or webroot lib/). The developer correctly discovered this and wrote a self-contained ~80-line STORE-only (no-compression) ZIP writer instead — which is LOWER supply-chain risk than adding a new lib. Reviewed that writer: standard CRC32, all DataView writes are in-bounds for the allocated buffers, fixed local-file-header + central-directory + EOCD structure, 32-bit fields with a documented no-ZIP64 rationale (realistic sizes <4GB), no eval/Function/document.write. Clean. index.html additions are static markup (tool tab + panel with a text range input value='all' and an aria-live status span) — no user data interpolated; app.js just imports+initBurst(). Deployment integrity: diff -rq repo web/ vs /var/www webroot is CLEAN (burst.js byte-identical). Full-JS audit across all 47 modules: every non-comment innerHTML assignment is an empty-string clear (= ''); zero eval/Function/document.write/insertAdjacentHTML/outerHTML with user content; zero fetch/XHR/importScripts/remote-src (100% client-side); secret-scan clean (prior 'const token' range-parser hits are false positives). upload.js still enforces ext+MIME+empty+size(50MB)+%PDF- magic with sanitized filenames. Live headers via curl: full resource CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; worker-src 'self' blob:; img-src 'self' blob: data:; connect-src 'self' blob:; frame-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; font-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. nginx -t OK.
